title: Add auto-refresh for live IBJA gold and silver prices

requirements.text: Update the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo to periodically fetch live gold and silver prices from the IBJA rates endpoint using HTTP outcalls on a 10-second recurring timer, exposing a query method that returns the latest fetched prices (or fallback values of Gold 24K ₹1,70,000/10g and Silver ₹3,09,000/kg when the API is unavailable or returns an error).

quotes: add live auto-refresh from the IBJA API on top of these fallback values

acceptanceCriteria: Backend actor fetches from the IBJA rates URL every 10 seconds via HTTP outcalls
A query method returns the most recently fetched gold and silver prices
When the IBJA API call fails or returns invalid data, the fallback values (Gold 24K ₹1,70,000/10g, Silver ₹3,09,000/kg) are returned instead
The transform function correctly processes IBJA HTTP responses

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to poll the backend canister actor for live prices using React Query with a 10-second refetch interval (as defined in frontend/src/config/refresh.ts), falling back to the hardcoded values in frontend/src/config/fallback.ts when the backend returns an error or unavailable prices.

quotes: auto-refresh functionality to pull live gold and silver prices from the IBJA API

acceptanceCriteria: useQueries.ts polls the backend actor query method every 10 seconds
Prices displayed in LiveRatesDashboard and MetalTicker update automatically without user interaction
Fallback values from frontend/src/config/fallback.ts are used when the backend call fails
Loading states and error states are handled gracefully

requirements.text: Update LiveRatesDashboard.tsx and MetalTicker.tsx to reflect auto-refreshing prices: show a visual indicator (e.g., a pulsing dot or 'Live' badge) when prices are being fetched from the IBJA API, and display the last-updated timestamp so users know when prices were last refreshed.

quotes: users always see the latest rates

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Live' or pulsing indicator is visible in LiveRatesDashboard and MetalTicker when auto-refresh is active
A last-updated timestamp is displayed and updates after each successful price fetch
The manual refresh button in LiveRatesDashboard still works and triggers an immediate re-fetch
Loading skeleton states appear during fetch intervals as before

constraints: Fallback prices must remain Gold 24K ₹1,70,000/10g and Silver ₹3,09,000/kg
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All backend logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor
nonGoals: Adding user authentication or login for price viewing
Changing the existing visual design or color theme
